page: Amazon Web Services m8g.24xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processors using the ARM64 architecture. It features 96 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz with a single thread per core. The processor configuration includes 64 KB of L1D cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It operates on the Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 40 Gbps. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance type.

In benchmark evaluations, the m8g.24xlarge demonstrates top-tier multi-core performance, ranking in the top 10% for stress-ng multi-core CPU tests, Geekbench multi-core scores, and PassMark CPU physics tests. Memory performance is also highly competitive, with top-tier results in both small-block cached and large-block uncached read/write memory bandwidth. Single-core performance is moderate, placing in the middle 50% for stress-ng single-core tests and the top 25% for Geekbench single-core evaluations. The server excels in multi-threaded decompression, software compilation, and static web serving throughput, all scoring in the top 10%.

This instance is highly suited for multi-threaded workloads, database operations, and high-throughput web serving, as evidenced by its top-tier Redis SET operations and PassMark database scores. It also shows strong capabilities in large-model LLM text generation. However, the lack of local NVMe storage requires relying on network-attached storage, which may introduce latency tradeoffs for I/O-heavy applications. The ARM64 architecture offers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for multi-core workloads, though single-threaded tasks do not leverage its full parallel processing potential.
description: The Amazon Web Services m8g.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the ARM64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. It features 96 dedicated physical cores operating at 2.8 GHz, paired with 384.0 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio. Running on the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 40 Gbps, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmarks highlight top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and superior throughput for database operations, software compilation, and static web serving. While single-core performance is moderate, the server offers excellent resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for highly parallelized workloads. It is highly suited for multi-threaded applications, Redis deployments, large-scale web serving, and medium-to-large LLM text generation tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8g.24xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose server with 96 Graviton cores and 384 GB RAM, delivering top-tier multi-core performance and memory bandwidth for databases and web serving.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8g.24xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose server featuring 96 Graviton cores, 384 GB RAM, and 40 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: A high-density ARM64 platform delivering exceptional multi-core performance and memory bandwidth for parallel workloads and database operations.
bullet_points: Powered by 96 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 ARM64 cores at 2.8 GHz
384.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database performance
40 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Best suited for multi-threaded applications, Redis, and web serving
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
